data.title: Lincoln National outlines continued group margin expansion and capital deployment plans through 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lincoln National Corporation (LNC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Ellen R. Gail Cooper, Chairman, President and CEO, reported strong performance for the quarter, highlighting "adjusted operating income increasing 32% year-over-year, underscoring the progress we have made as we advance our growth strategy with discipline and focus across Lincoln." Cooper emphasized four consecutive quarters of year-over-year adjusted operating income growth and noted "all 4 businesses deliver double-digit sales growth, a portion of which came from products that have historically not been key drivers for Lincoln." She also stated, "Group Protection delivered a record quarter for earnings and its highest-ever margin. Annuities generated its third highest sales quarter, supported by a more diverse and balanced product mix."

  • Cooper detailed evolving the company’s direction toward "increasing our risk-adjusted return on capital, reducing the volatility of our results and growing our franchise," citing concrete progress in optimizing the operating model and launching a Bermuda-based reinsurance subsidiary. She added, "Spread-based products comprised 2/3 of the overall [annuity] mix with fixed annuity sequential growth of 41% and RILA's sequential growth of 12%."

  • Cooper reported, "Life insurance achieved positive earnings driven by favorable mortality and improved expenses," and noted double-digit growth in both life and group businesses. She highlighted, "Group delivered another record quarter, and we are very pleased with the strategic momentum. Earnings grew by 33% year-over-year and the margin increased by 250 basis points to 12.5%."

  • Christopher Michael Neczypor, EVP & CFO, stated, "We reported second quarter adjusted operating income available to common stockholders of $427 million or $2.36 per diluted share." Neczypor added, "Net income available to common stockholders of $688 million or $3.80 per diluted share," clarifying the difference from operating income as "driven primarily by the positive movement in market risk benefits amid a stable interest rate environment and higher equity markets, partially offset by a decline in the value of our related hedge instruments."

Outlook

  • Neczypor indicated, "We expect our margin for the second half of 2025 to be broadly in line with the margin achieved during the second half of 2024," emphasizing the ongoing strategic expansion in the Group segment and continued investments in supplemental health and local markets.

  • Management reiterated confidence in "our ability to grow our free cash flow and deliver a more diversified higher risk-adjusted return set of earnings as a result," with capital deployment initiatives expected in the third and fourth quarters and some carrying into the end of next year.

Financial Results

  • Neczypor reported, "Our life loss ratio declined to 67.2% compared to 75.6% in the second quarter of 2024," and "the disability loss ratio also improved year-over-year, coming in at 64.2% compared with 65.9% in the prior year quarter."

  • Group Protection delivered "operating earnings of $173 million, up 33% from $130 million in the prior year second quarter."

  • Annuities posted "operating income of $287 million compared to operating income of $297 million in the prior year quarter," with spread-based products now representing 28% of total annuity account balances net of reinsurance.

  • Retirement Plan Services reported "operating earnings of $37 million compared to $40 million in the prior year quarter, but sequentially up from $34 million in the first quarter."

  • Life Insurance saw "operating earnings of $32 million for the second quarter reflecting substantial improvement compared to an operating loss of $35 million in the prior year quarter."

Q&A

  • Ryan Joel Krueger, KBW, asked about group margin expansion due to local markets and supplemental health. Cooper explained the "local market ... happens to be the highest margin and fastest-growing part of group," and Neczypor clarified, "We expected 100 basis points of margin improvement in 2025 from our strategic efforts ... we see a lot of runway in growing the supplemental health product portfolio and the local market."

  • Krueger inquired about restructuring life captives. Neczypor responded, "It's something we've been working on for a while ... the impact on earnings and free cash flow at a minimum would be next year, not this year."

  • Suneet Laxman L. Kamath, Jefferies, asked about RILA product sales and industry competition. Cooper noted, "We saw about a 32% increase in overall sales," and emphasized focus on profitable, capital-efficient growth.

  • Kamath also questioned free cash flow conversion guidance post-Bain Capital. Neczypor said, "We're feeling good about the outlook that we provided ... as we grow Group, as we're more efficient with Bermuda, as we're more efficient from an operating model ... all of those things would suggest a higher free cash flow conversion rate, but over time."

  • Taylor Alexander Scott, Barclays, asked about distribution and group growth drivers. Cooper emphasized investments in technology, digital tools, and strategic broker relationships.

  • Scott asked about external reinsurance. Neczypor stated, "It's too early for us to really talk about anything we're focused on there with real detail ... the environment is robust."

  • Wesley Collin Carmichael, Autonomous Research, asked about GAAP profitability and remeasurement gains. Neczypor attributed these to "favorability that's happening specifically in disability," driven by positive trends in reserves.

  • Carmichael also asked about Retirement Plan Services' sales pipeline. Cooper stated, "We can see that they actually are quite healthy ... we've seen some really nice first year of sales momentum and also recurring deposits as well."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally positive, focusing on margin expansion, sales growth, and strategic capital deployment, with pressing questions around long-term free cash flow, reinsurance, and competitive positioning.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases like "we remain confident in our strategic trajectory" and "our momentum is building."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased confidence, with analysts showing more focus on strategic execution and margin sustainability, while management reinforced their commitment to long-term growth and capital efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize sustained margin expansion in group and ongoing capital deployment, compared to the previous quarter's focus on macroeconomic uncertainty and Bain Capital transaction benefits.

  • Strategic focus remained on diversification, margin expansion, and capital efficiency, but current remarks provided more concrete updates on group margin improvement and capital deployment timing.

  • Analysts shifted from questions about partnership impacts and capital needs to more granular inquiries on margin drivers, product sales, and future capital actions.

  • Key metrics such as group operating earnings and life insurance earnings showed notable improvement, and management's tone became more assertive about the company's growth trajectory.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted possible volatility in mortality and disability outcomes but cited strong current trends. Neczypor said, "Should incidence rates revert towards historical levels, the anticipated margin headwind would remain."

  • The annual experience refund for one state's Paid Family leave program is now being accrued quarterly, which could introduce some variability.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing and magnitude of capital deployment and the environment for external reinsurance, but management emphasized ongoing review and robust market conditions.

Final Takeaway

Lincoln National showcased robust second quarter results, marked by strong group and life earnings, margin expansion, and progress in strategic capital allocation. Management reiterated confidence in their long-term growth trajectory, underpinned by a diversified product mix, disciplined execution, and ongoing investments in technology and operating efficiency. With a record group margin, improved life results, and clear capital deployment plans, the company positioned itself for continued value creation and resilient free cash flow growth through 2025 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Western Digital rises after 'strong' Q4; driven by 'strength in datacenter sales,' say analysts
data.description:
Shares of Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) rose about 6% on Thursday after fiscal fourth quarter results and first quarter outlook surpassed estimates, drawing positive views from analysts.

Wedbush kept its Outperform rating on Western Digital and raised the price target on the stock to $90 from $78.

"For the 2nd quarter in a row; WDC reported strong results and provided a better than consensus (but we also believe very beatable) outlook," said analysts led by Matt Bryson.

Specifically, the analysts said that Western Digital is seemingly set to benefit from continued favorable mix shift (driven in particular by the ramp of its industry leading 26TB CMR/32TB UltraSMR drives) amidst an extremely favorable industry backdrop (with supply trailing demand).

The analysts believe the company's gross margin, or GM, guidance (up 20 basis points at the midpoint) in particular looks extremely conservative, seemingly setting the company up for anotherbeat and raise quarter.

Bryson and his team noted that they do not see any reason to shift their constructive view on Western Digital.

Benchmark Equity Research maintained its Buy rating on Western Digital and increased the price target to $85 from $58.

"Driven by continued strength in datacenter sales Western Digital posted upside results for the June quarter and guided higher for the September quarter," said analyst Mark Miller.

The analysts added that Cloud sales represented 90% of total June quarter sales up from 87% in the prior quarter. Nearline drive shipments were up 17% sequentially and 36% year-over-year. The analysts see fiscal year 2026 non-GAAP earnings growing 23% year-over-year to $6.92 per diluted share.

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating on Western Digital and upped the price target to $92 from $83.

Analysts led by Erik Woodring said Western Digital results re-confirm Hard Disk Drive, or HDD, demand strength and supply tightness, helping to sustain stable pricing and elongate the HDD cycle.

The analysts expect some nitpicks on September quarter gross margin guidance, but added that the company management is likely being conservative, leaving open the potential for stronger EPS upside than modeled.

Woodring and his team noted that positive estimate revisions, a structural re-rating of HDD stocks, and closing the valuation discount with Seagate Technology (STX) are all likely to drive Western Digital's outperformance.

data.title: Moderna gets EU nod for updated COVID-19 vaccine
data.description:
• Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) has received approval from the European Commission for an updated version of its COVID-19 vaccine, Spikevax.
  • This updated vaccine targets the LP.8.1 variant and is intended for individuals aged six months and older to help prevent COVID-19.
  • The approval aligns with recommendations from global health organizations, which suggested updating COVID-19 vaccines to address the LP.8.1 strain for the upcoming 2025-2026 vaccination season.
  • This authorization is applicable in all 27 EU member countries, along with Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway.
  • Doses will be ready for eligible groups before the 2025-2026 vaccination season begins, the company said.
